hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model is trained to align SMILES strings with 3D electron density grids, then the resulting 3D embeddings will be emergently invariant to molecular rotations, yielding higher cross-rotation retrieval accuracy, greater multi-pose retrieval rates and pose diversity, improved functional-group-wise recall and clustering (lower Davies–Bouldin index), and stronger linear-probe property prediction on QM9 compared to unimodal or non-contrastive baselines.
